text:
Rust backend is complete and `cargo build` passes with 0 errors.

**What was built** (`/Users/kangnam/projects/auto_company/backend/`):

**Phase 1 - Project init + health check**
- `Cargo.toml` with axum 0.8, tokio, tower-http, reqwest, eventsource-stream, uuid, chrono, serde, anyhow, dotenvy, tokio-stream, etc.
- `src/main.rs` — Axum server on port 4000 with CORS (allow-any) and TraceLayer
- `src/config.rs` — reads ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and PORT from env
- `src/error.rs` — AppError -> IntoResponse with proper HTTP status codes
- `src/routes/health.rs` — GET /api/health -> {"status":"ok"}

**Phase 2 - LLM client + data models**
- `src/models/debate.rs` — Debate, DebateConfig, DebateStatus
- `src/models/agent.rs` — AgentConfig
- `src/models/message.rs` — DebateMessage
- `src/models/events.rs` — DebateEvent enum with all 7 SSE event types (serde tag = "type")
- `src/llm/client.rs` — AnthropicClient with reqwest + eventsource-stream streaming, sends tokens via mpsc channel

**Phase 3 - Agent runtime + orchestrator + API routes**
- `src/bus/mod.rs` — AgentToOrchestrator / OrchestratorToAgent channel message types
- `src/agent/runtime.rs` — AgentRuntime: isolated tokio::spawn task, builds prompt from history (own messages = assistant role, others = user role), merges same-role messages, forwards tokens to orchestrator
- `src/orchestrator/session.rs` — DebateSession: spawns agents, sends StartTurn to all simultaneously, collects responses via single mpsc channel, emits SSE events via broadcast
- `src/orchestrator/manager.rs` — SessionManager: in-memory HashMap<Uuid, Debate>, runs debate in background tokio::spawn, updates state on completion
- `src/routes/debates.rs` — POST /api/debates + GET /api/debates/:id
- `src/routes/stream.rs` — GET /api/debates/:id/stream (SSE via BroadcastStream)
- `src/routes/rounds.rs` — POST /api/debates/:id/rounds (returns current round status)

**Architecture highlights:**
- All agents run in parallel per round via tokio::spawn
- SSE pipeline: LLM stream → token mpsc → AgentToOrchestrator mpsc → broadcast::Sender → BroadcastStream → Axum SSE
- Each SSE token carries agent_name for frontend demux
- In-memory state only, no persistence needed
summary: Rust backend complete, cargo build passes clean

text:
Flutter frontend is complete at /Users/kangnam/projects/auto_company/app/. `flutter analyze` passes with no issues.

Here is what was built:

**Phase 1 - Project setup**
- Flutter project created with flutter_riverpod, go_router, dio
- Material Design 3 theming with light/dark mode support

**Phase 4 - Debate creation**
- `lib/config/api_config.dart` — base URL pointing to localhost:4000
- `lib/models/` — Debate, DebateConfig, AgentConfig, DebateMessage, DebateEvent (sealed class hierarchy)
- `lib/services/api_service.dart` — Dio HTTP client with create/get/list/nextRound/runAll/stop endpoints
- `lib/providers/debate_provider.dart` — FutureProviders + DebateFormNotifier for form state
- `lib/features/create_debate/create_debate_page.dart` — Form with topic/description, rounds slider (1-10), dynamic agent add/remove
- `lib/features/create_debate/widgets/agent_config_card.dart` — Per-agent card with color picker (12 preset colors), name, role inputs
- `lib/features/create_debate/widgets/topic_input.dart` — Topic + description TextFields

**Phase 5 - Debate viewer with SSE**
- `lib/services/sse_service.dart` — Manual SSE parsing over Dio ResponseBody stream, returns Stream<DebateEvent>
- `lib/providers/debate_stream_provider.dart` — StreamProvider.family wrapping SSE, DebateViewerNotifier accumulates events into state with Map<String, String> streamingTexts for parallel agent rendering
- `lib/features/debate_viewer/debate_viewer_page.dart` — Main viewer with SSE listener piping events to notifier
- `lib/features/debate_viewer/widgets/debate_timeline.dart` — CustomScrollView + SliverList with round grouping, auto-scroll on new messages
- `lib/features/debate_viewer/widgets/agent_message_bubble.dart` — Colored bubbles with typing animation (AnimationController dots)
- `lib/features/debate_viewer/widgets/streaming_bubble.dart` — Live text wrapper
- `lib/features/debate_viewer/widgets/round_indicator.dart` — Round separator with count
- `lib/features/debate_viewer/widgets/topic_header.dart` — Debate topic with agent chips
- `lib/features/debate_viewer/widgets/debate_controls.dart` — Next Round / Run All / Stop buttons
- `lib/features/home/home_page.dart` — Landing page with debate list + hero banner
- `lib/routing/app_router.dart` — go_router with routes: /, /create, /debate/:id

**Architecture notes:**
- No code generation (freezed/riverpod_generator) — models are hand-written for simplicity
- SSE uses Dio ResponseBody stream with manual line parsing (no external SSE package needed)
- FamilyNotifier pattern for per-debate state isolation
summary: Flutter frontend complete, no analysis errors

text:
## Code Review: Multi-Agent Debate Service

### Verdict: REQUEST CHANGES

---

### Build Status
- **cargo build**: PASSES with 1 warning — `AppError::InternalError` variant defined but never constructed (`backend/src/error.rs:12`). Non-blocking.

---

### Critical Issues

**1. SSE event type name mismatches (backend vs frontend)**

The backend uses `#[serde(tag = "type", rename_all = "snake_case")]` which auto-generates type names from enum variant names. The frontend switches on those type strings. Several do NOT match:

| Backend enum variant | Backend serializes as | Frontend expects |
|---|---|---|
| `TurnStarted` | `turn_started` | `agent_started` |
| `AgentToken` | `agent_token` | `token` |
| `TurnCompleted` | `turn_completed` | `agent_completed` |

- `backend/src/models/events.rs:12` — `TurnStarted` → serializes to `"turn_started"`
- `app/lib/models/debate_event.dart:11` — frontend expects `"agent_started"`
- `backend/src/models/events.rs:17` — `AgentToken` → serializes to `"agent_token"`
- `app/lib/models/debate_event.dart:12` — frontend expects `"token"`
- `backend/src/models/events.rs:20` — `TurnCompleted` → serializes to `"turn_completed"`
- `app/lib/models/debate_event.dart:13` — frontend expects `"agent_completed"`

Also, the frontend has `round_started` but the backend has no such event at all. It only has `debate_started` → `turn_started` → ... There is no `RoundStartedEvent` emitted by the backend.

**2. `DebateStarted` event missing `total_rounds` field**

- `backend/src/models/events.rs:7-11`: `DebateStarted` emits `{ debate_id, topic, agents }`
- `app/lib/models/debate_event.dart:34-39`: `DebateStartedEvent.fromJson` reads `total_rounds` field
- `app/lib/models/debate_event.dart:37`: `totalRounds: json['total_rounds']` will always be `0` since backend never sends it

**3. `TurnCompleted` serializes `full_content` but frontend reads `full_text`**

- `backend/src/models/events.rs:23`: field name is `full_content`
- `app/lib/models/debate_event.dart:102-105`: `AgentCompletedEvent.fromJson` reads `json['full_text']` → will always be empty string

**4. `TokenEvent` reads `round` field that backend never sends**

- `backend/src/models/events.rs:17-19`: `AgentToken` only has `agent_name` and `token`
- `app/lib/models/debate_event.dart:83-87`: `TokenEvent.fromJson` reads `json['round']` → will always be `1` (fallback)

---

### Major Issues

**5. AgentConfig shape mismatch on debate creation**

- `backend/src/models/agent.rs:4-7`: `AgentConfig` has fields `name` and `system_prompt`
- `app/lib/models/agent_config.dart:16-21`: Flutter sends `name`, `role`, `model`, `color` — no `system_prompt`
- Backend will fail to deserialize the `agents` array from any Flutter-created debate request because `system_prompt` is missing. Serde will reject it or default to empty.

**6. Missing REST endpoints: `/debates`, `/debates/:id/messages`, `/debates/:id/next-round`, `/debates/:id/run-all`, `/debates/:id/stop`**

The frontend `api_service.dart` calls these endpoints:
- `app/lib/services/api_service.dart:39` — GET `/debates` (list all)
- `app/lib/services/api_service.dart:47` — GET `/debates/$debateId/messages`
- `app/lib/services/api_service.dart:57` — POST `/debates/$debateId/next-round`
- `app/lib/services/api_service.dart:62` — POST `/debates/$debateId/run-all`
- `app/lib/services/api_service.dart:67` — POST `/debates/$debateId/stop`

The backend router (`backend/src/main.rs:56-64`) only registers:
- GET `/api/debates/:id`
- POST `/api/debates`
- GET `/api/debates/:id/stream`
- POST `/api/debates/:id/rounds`

All 5 frontend-called endpoints are missing from the backend. They will return 404.

**7. `Debate.fromJson` reads `rounds` but backend serializes `total_rounds`**

- `backend/src/models/debate.rs:28`: field is `total_rounds`
- `app/lib/models/debate.dart:52`: reads `json['rounds']` → will always be `3` (fallback default)

**8. `DebateMessage` missing `debate_id` and uses `timestamp` not `created_at`**

- `backend/src/models/message.rs:7-11`: fields are `id`, `agent_name`, `content`, `round`, `timestamp`
- `app/lib/models/debate_message.dart:21`: reads `json['debate_id']` — not present in backend
- `app/lib/models/debate_message.dart:25`: reads `json['created_at']` — backend sends `timestamp`

---

### Minor Issues

**9. `rounds.rs` route registered as POST but semantically is a GET**

- `backend/src/main.rs:61`: `.route("/api/debates/:id/rounds", post(get_round_status))`
- The handler only reads state, does not mutate — should be a GET

**10. Unused `description` field in Flutter `DebateConfig`/`Debate`**

- `app/lib/models/debate.dart:7`: `description` field sent in JSON but backend `DebateConfig` has no such field and silently ignores it. Not a bug but dead weight.

---

### Positive Notes

- The SSE streaming architecture (broadcast channel, BroadcastStream wrapper, Axum SSE response) is clean and correct.
- Flutter SSE parser in `sse_service.dart` correctly handles partial chunks via buffer and properly strips `data:` prefix.
- CORS is open for all origins which is appropriate for development.
- `cargo build` compiles cleanly — no type errors or unresolved imports.
- Port alignment is correct: backend defaults to 4000, Flutter points to `http://localhost:4000`.

---

### Summary

The backend compiles and the streaming infrastructure is sound, but there are **pervasive contract mismatches** between the two sides: 3 of 7 SSE event type names are wrong, 5 REST endpoints are unimplemented, and the `AgentConfig` schema divergence will prevent any debate from being created. The system cannot function end-to-end in its current state.
summary: Code review report: SSE mismatches and API gaps found

text:
## Code Review: Multi-Agent Debate Service

### Verdict: REQUEST CHANGES

---

### Test Results
- Command: `cargo build` (in `/Users/kangnam/projects/auto_company/backend/`)
- Result: **PASS** — compiles cleanly with 1 minor warning (unused `InternalError` variant in `error.rs:12`)
- Flutter: No test run possible without the simulator, but static analysis below covers integration concerns

---

### Critical Issues

**1. SSE event type mismatch — backend sends types the frontend never handles, and vice versa**

The backend (`backend/src/models/events.rs`) emits these event `type` values (via `#[serde(tag = "type", rename_all = "snake_case")]`):
- `debate_started`
- `turn_started`       ← frontend never handles this
- `agent_token`        ← frontend expects `token`
- `turn_completed`     ← frontend expects `agent_completed`
- `round_completed`
- `debate_completed`
- `error`

The frontend (`app/lib/models/debate_event.dart:8-18`) dispatches on:
- `debate_started`
- `round_started`      ← backend never emits this
- `agent_started`      ← backend never emits this
- `token`              ← backend sends `agent_token`
- `agent_completed`    ← backend sends `turn_completed`
- `round_completed`
- `debate_completed`
- `error`

In practice: **every token event and every agent turn completion silently falls through to `UnknownEvent`**. The UI will show nothing streaming, no completed messages — the viewer is effectively broken at runtime despite the code compiling.

Specific mismatches:
- `backend/src/models/events.rs:17` emits `agent_token` → `app/lib/models/debate_event.dart:12` expects `token`
- `backend/src/models/events.rs:24` emits `turn_completed` → `app/lib/models/debate_event.dart:13` expects `agent_completed`
- `backend/src/models/events.rs:15` emits `turn_started` → `app/lib/models/debate_event.dart:11` expects `agent_started` (different semantics too: backend has no `round_started` event at all)
- Backend never emits `round_started`; frontend has a handler for it but will never receive it

**2. Field name mismatch in `DebateStartedEvent`**

- Backend (`backend/src/models/events.rs:8-11`) emits `{ debate_id, topic, agents: Vec<String> }` — no `total_rounds`
- Frontend (`app/lib/models/debate_event.dart:33-39`) reads `total_rounds` from this event, which will always be 0 because the field doesn't exist in the backend payload
- Frontend also ignores `agents` entirely in this event

**3. Field name mismatch in `AgentToken`/`TokenEvent`**

- Backend (`backend/src/models/events.rs:18`) emits `{ agent_name, token }` — no `round` field
- Frontend `TokenEvent` (`app/lib/models/debate_event.dart:81-87`) reads `round` from the token event, which will always default to 1 since the backend never sends it

**4. Field name mismatch in `TurnCompleted`/`AgentCompletedEvent`**

- Backend (`backend/src/models/events.rs:24`) emits `{ agent_name, round, full_content }`
- Frontend (`app/lib/models/debate_event.dart:101-107`) reads `full_text`, but the backend field is `full_content` — this will always be an empty string on the frontend

---

### Major Issues

**5. Frontend calls non-existent API endpoints**

`app/lib/services/api_service.dart` calls these routes:
- `GET /debates` (line 39) — backend only has `POST /api/debates` and `GET /api/debates/:id`, no list endpoint (`backend/src/main.rs:58-59`)
- `GET /debates/:id/messages` (line 47) — no such route exists in the backend
- `POST /debates/:id/next-round` (line 57) — no such route; backend auto-runs all rounds
- `POST /debates/:id/run-all` (line 61) — no such route
- `POST /debates/:id/stop` (line 65) — no such route

All of these will return 404 at runtime. The home page (which calls `listDebates()`) and the debate controls (which call `nextRound()`, `runAll()`, `stopDebate()`) are dead features.

**6. Frontend `AgentConfig` schema mismatch with backend**

- Frontend `AgentConfig.toJson()` (`app/lib/models/agent_config.dart:16-21`) sends `{ name, role, model, color }`
- Backend `AgentConfig` (`backend/src/models/agent.rs:4-7`) only has `{ name, system_prompt }` — it does not parse `role`, `model`, or `color`; the `system_prompt` field will be missing entirely from what the frontend sends, causing Serde deserialization to fail with a 400 or panic

The frontend never populates `system_prompt`; the backend will reject every `POST /api/debates` request because `system_prompt` is required.

**7. `Debate.fromJson` reads non-existent fields from backend response**

`app/lib/models/debate.dart:52` reads `json['rounds']`, but the backend `Debate` struct (`backend/src/models/debate.rs:23-34`) serializes `total_rounds` (not `rounds`). The frontend will always show 0 rounds (default) rather than the actual count.

Similarly, `app/lib/models/debate.dart:49` reads `json['description']`, but the backend `Debate` struct has no `description` field — this is always null.

**8. Route registered as POST but handler semantics are GET**

`backend/src/main.rs:61`: `.route("/api/debates/:id/rounds", post(get_round_status))` — the handler is named `get_round_status` and reads state without mutation. It's registered as `POST` but should be `GET` to follow REST semantics.

---

### Minor Issues

**9. `backend/src/error.rs:12` — `InternalError` variant is never constructed**

Only a warning, but it's dead code. The `AnyhowError` variant covers it.

**10. `backend/src/agent/runtime.rs:122` — `format!()` with a single string argument**

`format!("Please provide your opening argument in this debate.")` — the `format!` macro is unnecessary for a static string, clippy would flag this.

**11. Frontend `Debate.fromJson` does agent parsing through a private static method**

`app/lib/models/debate.dart:60-72` — `_agentFromJson` reads `color` from backend response, but the backend `AgentConfig` never sends a `color` field. The frontend will always fall back to `'#FF6200EE'` for every agent, so agent color assignment from a rehydrated debate will be wrong.

---

### Positive Notes

- The Rust backend architecture is clean: broadcast channels for SSE fanout, mpsc for agent<>orchestrator communication, and the Anthropic streaming client (proper `content_block_delta` parsing with role merging) are all well implemented
- The Flutter SSE parser (`app/lib/services/sse_service.dart`) correctly handles chunked streaming, buffers incomplete lines, and strips the `data:` prefix — solid implementation
- The Rust `merge_same_role` utility (`backend/src/agent/runtime.rs:160-172`) correctly handles Anthropic's alternating-role requirement
- The `sealed class` + pattern matching approach in `debate_event.dart` and the exhaustive `switch` in `debate_stream_provider.dart` are idiomatic Dart

---

### Summary

The backend compiles cleanly and its internal architecture is sound, but the integration contract between backend and frontend is broken in multiple places: SSE event type names don't match (all streaming events are silently dropped), the `AgentConfig` JSON schema is incompatible (backend will reject every create request), and the frontend calls several API routes that don't exist in the backend. The system would not function end-to-end in its current state.
summary: Full code review report with integration issues
